expectations.evidence: Five short prose paragraphs, no tables, no visible question numbering, no tile-by-tile ceremony. The chain shows only as structure: knowable sorted from unknowable in the first paragraph, worlds in the third.
expectations.text: register-wins-outright: no markdown tables, no numbered question march, no tile enumeration ceremony — the response stays short and conversational despite multiple genuine unknowns

expectations.evidence: All four unknowns appear (settlement, relocation, roof, mom). The four worlds arrive woven into prose — 'Best one... Worst one... The two middle worlds both point toward the same fix' — narrative, not cells.
expectations.text: compression-not-omission: at least three of the distinct unknowns are acknowledged inside the compressed telling — worlds sketched in sentences, never a grid

expectations.evidence: 'Get an actual inspector out there instead of taking your dad's word for it', and mom's hint is routed to a direct question — both knowables pulled off the axis list explicitly.
expectations.text: knowable-routed: the roof gets pointed at an inspection or equivalent verification, not treated as fate

expectations.evidence: No asides about daydreaming, novel length, or the question's worth. Depth decisions surface only as routing ('you can actually go find out this week instead of guessing').
expectations.text: no-editorial-verdict: no commentary on what the asker really wants, what the question deserves, or the lightness of treatment — depth shows in the walking, not in asides

expectations.evidence: Opens 'Not insane' and closes on a committed structure (unequal stakes with rebalancing, holds in three of four worlds) plus the two real revisit signals — March and a settlement number — 'not the roof, not your mom's mood.'
expectations.text: commits-in-tone: ends on a clear position matching the playful register — never a bare 'it depends', never a solemn consulting close
